_meta.description: Structured knowledge base of Hadean Earth research papers with extracted visual constraints for image prompt generation.
_meta.usage: Feed this to paper_to_prompt_rag.py to generate literature-grounded image prompts.

papers.title: The Hadean Crust: Evidence from >4 Ga Zircons
papers.journal: Annual Review of Earth and Planetary Sciences

visual_constraints.surface: dark oceanic surface with protocrustal fragments
visual_constraints.water: possible liquid water ocean, not purely molten
visual_constraints.continents: no modern continents; scattered protocrustal islands
visual_constraints.palette: dark blues, greys, muted orange glow from volcanic activity

papers.key_finding: Zircon evidence suggests liquid water existed on Earth's surface as early as 4.4 Ga, challenging the 'hellish Hadean' narrative.

papers.authors: Hopkins, M., Harrison, T.M., Manning, C.E.
papers.title: Low heat flow inferred from >4 Gyr zircons suggests Hadean plate boundary interactions

visual_constraints.surface: fractured protocrust with localized volcanic belts
visual_constraints.water: possible shallow seas between crustal fragments
visual_constraints.palette: dark basalt, faint red volcanic seams, grey-brown crust

papers.key_finding: Low heat flow suggests early plate-boundary-like interactions, meaning the Hadean was not simply a global magma ocean.

papers.authors: Zahnle, K., Lupu, R., Catling, D., Wogan, N.
papers.title: Creation and Evolution of Impact-generated Reduced Atmospheres of Early Earth

visual_constraints.atmosphere: thick reducing atmosphere with H2, CH4, NH3, HCN after large impacts
visual_constraints.sky_color: orange-brown haze, not blue; possibly reddish during impact aftermaths
visual_constraints.impact_effects: steam plumes, vaporized rock, impact-generated fog
visual_constraints.palette: orange, brown, dark red, grey haze

papers.key_finding: Large impacts created transient reduced atmospheres rich in hydrogen and methane, suitable for prebiotic chemistry.

papers.authors: Martin, W., Baross, J., Kelley, D., Russell, M.J.
papers.title: Hydrothermal vents and the origin of life

visual_constraints.setting: deep ocean floor with hydrothermal vent chimneys
visual_constraints.vents: black smokers emitting dark mineral-rich plumes
visual_constraints.water: turbulent mixing of superheated and cold water
visual_constraints.light: dim or no natural light, bioluminescence-free
visual_constraints.palette: deep black, mineral white/yellow chimney, dark green-black water

papers.key_finding: Alkaline hydrothermal vents provided chemical gradients and mineral catalysts for the origin of life.

papers.title: Formation and composition of Earth's Hadean protocrust

visual_constraints.surface: differentiated protocrust, not a uniform magma ocean
visual_constraints.crust_type: mafic to intermediate composition, darker than modern continents
visual_constraints.texture: rough, fractured, partially solidified crustal plates
visual_constraints.palette: dark grey, charcoal, faint reddish undertones from residual heat

papers.key_finding: Early Earth's protocrust was compositionally diverse, challenging the simple magma-ocean model.

papers.title: Growth of continental crust and lithosphere subduction in the Hadean revealed by geochemistry and geodynamics

visual_constraints.surface: structured crustal landscape with early recycling zones
visual_constraints.tectonics: primitive subduction-like boundaries, not modern plate tectonics
visual_constraints.palette: dark crust, orange-red volcanic lines, smoky haze

papers.key_finding: Continental crust growth and lithosphere recycling began in the Hadean.

papers.authors: Mojzsis, S.J., Harrison, T.M., Pidgeon, R.T.
papers.title: Oxygen-isotope evidence from ancient zircons for liquid water at the Earth's surface 4,300 Myr ago

visual_constraints.water: liquid water on Earth's surface at 4.3 Ga
visual_constraints.surface: dark rocky terrain with pools or shallow seas
visual_constraints.sky: overcast, thick atmosphere, no blue sky
visual_constraints.palette: dark water, grey-black rock, hazy yellowish sky

papers.key_finding: Oxygen isotopes in 4.3 Ga zircons indicate liquid water interaction, pushing ocean formation back into the deep Hadean.

papers.authors: Wilde, S.A., Valley, J.W., Peck, W.H., Graham, C.M.
papers.title: Evidence from detrital zircons for the existence of continental crust and oceans on the Earth 4.4 Gyr ago

visual_constraints.water: ocean present as early as 4.4 Ga
visual_constraints.surface: small protocontinental islands amid a dark global ocean
visual_constraints.palette: deep dark ocean, grey rocky islands, heavy overcast

papers.key_finding: Jack Hills zircons demonstrate continental crust and oceans existed at 4.4 Ga — only ~150 Myr after Earth formed.

papers.authors: Sleep, N.H., Zahnle, K., Neuhoff, P.S.
papers.title: Initiation of clement surface conditions on the earliest Earth
papers.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

visual_constraints.surface: relatively calm ocean surface, not perpetually boiling
visual_constraints.temperature: warm but not always molten; occasional calm periods
visual_constraints.sky: heavy greenish-grey cloud cover from CO2
visual_constraints.palette: warm grey ocean, thick cloud layers, subdued light

papers.key_finding: Clement (mild) surface conditions could have been established surprisingly early through CO2 weathering feedbacks.

papers.title: The Hadean-Archaean Environment
papers.journal: Cold Spring Harbor Perspectives in Biology

visual_constraints.surface: diverse: from magma ocean to temperate ocean over Hadean span
visual_constraints.bombardment: episodic heavy impacts causing temporary re-melting
visual_constraints.palette: transitional: bright orange-red early → dark blue-grey late

papers.key_finding: Comprehensive review showing Hadean-Archaean transition involved dramatic environmental shifts, not a single state.

papers.authors: Marchi, S., Bottke, W.F., Elkins-Tanton, L.T., et al.
papers.title: Widespread mixing and burial of Earth's Hadean crust by asteroid impacts

visual_constraints.impacts: frequent large asteroid impacts creating vast melt pools
visual_constraints.surface: heavily cratered, mixed and overturned crust
visual_constraints.melt: impact melt sheets stretching hundreds of kilometers
visual_constraints.palette: glowing orange melt pools, dark cratered terrain, debris-choked atmosphere

papers.key_finding: Asteroid impacts were so frequent and large that they repeatedly mixed and buried early crust, explaining why almost no Hadean rock survives.

papers.title: Magma Oceans in the Inner Solar System
papers.journal: Annual Review of Earth and Planetary Sciences

visual_constraints.surface: global magma ocean, glowing red-orange
visual_constraints.solidification: dark solidified rafts floating on magma
visual_constraints.atmosphere: thick steam atmosphere from degassing magma ocean
visual_constraints.sky: orange-red glow reflecting off low clouds
visual_constraints.palette: incandescent orange, deep red, black solidified crust, white-hot cracks

papers.key_finding: Earth's earliest phase involved a global magma ocean that solidified from the bottom up over ~few million years.

papers.title: Forming a Moon with an Earth-like Composition via a Giant Impact

visual_constraints.event: catastrophic collision between proto-Earth and Theia
visual_constraints.debris: massive debris disk surrounding shattered Earth
visual_constraints.surface: completely molten, vaporized rock atmosphere
visual_constraints.palette: white-hot impact zone, orange debris field, black space, glowing accretion disk

papers.key_finding: The Moon-forming impact was so energetic it likely vaporized a significant fraction of both bodies.

papers.authors: Bottke, W.F., Vokrouhlický, D., Minton, D., et al.
papers.title: An Archaean heavy bombardment from a destabilized extension of the asteroid belt

visual_constraints.impacts: sustained bombardment over hundreds of millions of years
visual_constraints.sky: frequent bright streaks of incoming bolides
visual_constraints.surface: scarred, cratered dark terrain
visual_constraints.palette: dark terrain, bright impact flashes, smoky atmosphere

papers.key_finding: Late Heavy Bombardment may have extended from a destabilized inner asteroid belt, not just outer solar system migration.

papers.authors: Trail, D., Watson, E.B., Tailby, N.D.
papers.title: The oxidation state of Hadean magmas and implications for early Earth's atmosphere

visual_constraints.volcanic_gases: CO2 and water vapor dominant, some SO2
visual_constraints.sky: dense, hazy, grey-orange tones from volcanic emissions
visual_constraints.surface: volcanic terrain with fumaroles and gas vents
visual_constraints.palette: grey volcanic fog, orange sulfur tints, dark basalt

papers.key_finding: Hadean magmas were similar in oxidation state to modern ones, suggesting the atmosphere was not as extremely reducing as some models predict.

papers.authors: Russell, M.J., Barge, L.M., Bhartia, R., et al.
papers.title: The Drive to Life on Wet and Icy Worlds

visual_constraints.setting: alkaline hydrothermal mound on ocean floor
visual_constraints.structures: porous mineral chimneys with internal chemical gradients
visual_constraints.fluid: warm alkaline fluid mixing with acidic ocean water
visual_constraints.minerals: iron-nickel sulfide, green rust, brucite precipitates
visual_constraints.palette: pale mineral towers, dark surrounding water, faint mineral glow

papers.key_finding: Alkaline hydrothermal vents (like Lost City) provide the chemical disequilibrium and mineral catalysts needed to drive the origin of life.

papers.title: Archean geodynamics and the thermal evolution of Earth

visual_constraints.interior: vigorously convecting mantle beneath thin crust
visual_constraints.surface: thin, mobile crustal plates over hot mantle
visual_constraints.volcanism: widespread but not necessarily more voluminous than today
visual_constraints.palette: cross-section view: red-orange mantle, dark thin crust, volcanic punctures

papers.key_finding: Contrary to intuition, a hotter Hadean mantle may have produced LESS vigorous plate tectonics due to dehydration stiffening.

papers.authors: Valley, J.W., Peck, W.H., King, E.M., Wilde, S.A.
papers.title: A cool early Earth

visual_constraints.surface: temperate to warm, not necessarily hellish
visual_constraints.water: stable liquid water, potentially widespread oceans
visual_constraints.landscape: dark rocky terrain with calm water bodies
visual_constraints.palette: muted dark blues, grey rock, overcast white-grey sky

papers.key_finding: Landmark 'Cool Early Earth' hypothesis: zircon oxygen isotopes suggest surface temperatures were moderate enough for liquid water at 4.4 Ga.

papers.title: The habitat and nature of early life

visual_constraints.setting: hydrothermal environments as cradles for early life
visual_constraints.surface: warm shallow pools near volcanic vents
visual_constraints.palette: warm mineral-stained pools, dark rock, steam wisps

papers.key_finding: Early life likely emerged in hydrothermal settings, possibly both deep-sea vents and shallow volcanic hot springs.

papers.authors: Tarduno, J.A., Cottrell, R.D., Davis, W.J., et al.
papers.title: A Hadean to Paleoarchean geodynamo recorded by single zircon crystals

visual_constraints.magnetic_field: early Earth had a magnetic field, providing some solar wind shielding
visual_constraints.aurora: possible intense auroral displays at poles
visual_constraints.atmosphere: partially shielded from solar wind stripping
visual_constraints.palette: dark sky with faint green/red auroral glow at horizon

papers.key_finding: Zircon paleomagnetic records suggest Earth had a geodynamo (magnetic field) as early as 4.2 Ga.

papers.title: How life began on Earth: a status report

visual_constraints.setting: diverse prebiotic environments: tidal pools, hydrothermal vents, impact craters with ponds
visual_constraints.chemistry: organic molecules accumulating in shallow warm pools
visual_constraints.palette: dark pools with mineral deposits, lightning-lit sky, steam

papers.key_finding: Multiple environments may have contributed to prebiotic chemistry, not just a single 'warm little pond'.

papers.title: Enhanced atmospheric loss on protoplanets at the giant impact phase in the presence of oceans

visual_constraints.event: giant impact blowing off atmosphere and ocean material
visual_constraints.debris: water vapor and atmosphere ejected into space
visual_constraints.surface: exposed magma beneath stripped ocean
visual_constraints.space_view: visible shock wave expanding from impact point
visual_constraints.palette: white-hot impact, blue-white atmosphere being blown away, dark space

papers.key_finding: Giant impacts could strip away oceans and atmospheres, meaning early Earth may have repeatedly lost and regained its volatile envelope.

papers.title: Thermal and chemical evolution of the terrestrial magma ocean
papers.journal: Physics of the Earth and Planetary Interiors

visual_constraints.surface: global magma ocean gradually solidifying
visual_constraints.crust_formation: thin dark crust forming and breaking on magma surface
visual_constraints.degassing: massive volcanic outgassing creating steam atmosphere
visual_constraints.palette: incandescent orange-red magma, black floating crust, white steam clouds

papers.key_finding: The magma ocean solidified relatively quickly (~few Myr), with volatile outgassing creating a thick steam atmosphere.

papers.title: Earth's Early Atmosphere

visual_constraints.atmosphere: thick CO2/N2 atmosphere with no free O2
visual_constraints.sky_color: pale orange to yellowish, not blue
visual_constraints.greenhouse: strong greenhouse warming, warm surface despite fainter Sun
visual_constraints.uv: intense UV at surface due to no ozone layer
visual_constraints.palette: orange-yellow hazy sky, warm grey ocean, faint Sun disk

papers.key_finding: Classic paper establishing early Earth's atmosphere was CO2/N2-dominated with strong greenhouse effect compensating for the faint young Sun.

papers.authors: Goldblatt, C., Claire, M.W., Lenton, T.M., et al.
papers.title: Nitrogen-enhanced greenhouse warming on early Earth

visual_constraints.atmosphere: thick N2-CO2 atmosphere, denser than today
visual_constraints.light_scattering: more Rayleigh scattering but overwhelmed by CO2 absorption
visual_constraints.sky: hazy, potentially whitish-orange from thick atmosphere
visual_constraints.palette: dense hazy horizon, diffused sunlight, warm-toned sky

papers.key_finding: Higher nitrogen partial pressure in early atmosphere enhanced greenhouse warming, helping solve the faint young Sun paradox.

papers.title: Endogenous production, exogenous delivery and impact-shock synthesis of organic molecules: an inventory for the origins of life

visual_constraints.event: comets and meteorites delivering organic material to early Earth
visual_constraints.surface: impact sites with organic-rich melt pools
visual_constraints.palette: dark impact pools, organic-brown deposits, hazy atmosphere

papers.key_finding: Organic molecules on early Earth came from multiple sources: atmospheric synthesis, hydrothermal vents, and extraterrestrial delivery via impacts.

papers.title: The nitrogen budget of Earth

visual_constraints.atmosphere: possibly 2-3x modern atmospheric pressure from nitrogen alone
visual_constraints.sky: dense, creating strong light scattering near horizon
visual_constraints.ocean: nitrogen dissolved in early ocean affecting color
visual_constraints.palette: thick atmosphere haze, compressed horizon, dense air shimmer

papers.key_finding: Early Earth may have had 2-3x the modern atmospheric mass from nitrogen alone, affecting surface pressure and climate.

papers.title: The evolving nature of terrestrial crust from the Hadean, through the Archaean, into the Proterozoic

visual_constraints.surface: evolving crust: ultramafic (very dark, dense) → mafic → felsic over time
visual_constraints.color: Hadean crust was darker (ultramafic komatiite-like) than modern continents
visual_constraints.palette: very dark green-black ultramafic rock, dark sea

papers.key_finding: Hadean crust was compositionally distinct from modern continents — darker, denser, and more mafic.

papers.authors: Debaille, V., O'Neill, C., Brandon, A.D., et al.
papers.title: Stagnant-lid tectonics in early Earth revealed by 142Nd variations in late Archean rocks

visual_constraints.tectonics: single-plate (stagnant lid) rather than modern plate tectonics
visual_constraints.surface: one continuous dark crust, punctuated by volcanic hotspots
visual_constraints.volcanism: plume-driven volcanoes rather than subduction arc chains
visual_constraints.palette: monotone dark surface, isolated volcanic plumes, no mountain ranges

papers.key_finding: Early Earth may have had stagnant-lid tectonics (like Venus today) rather than modern-style plate tectonics.

papers.title: Processes on the Young Earth and the Habitats of Early Life
papers.journal: Annual Review of Earth and Planetary Sciences

visual_constraints.surface: komatiite lava flows — extremely hot, fluid, fast-spreading
visual_constraints.volcanism: more widespread and voluminous eruptions than modern Earth
visual_constraints.ocean: hot ocean (possibly 60-80°C), greenish-tinted from dissolved iron
visual_constraints.palette: dark komatiite flows, greenish warm ocean, smoky volcanic sky

papers.key_finding: Early Earth volcanism produced komatiites (ultra-hot lavas), and oceans may have been significantly warmer and iron-rich.

orbit.description: View of Hadean Earth from space
orbit.composite_constraints: Dark planet with scattered volcanic glow, thick hazy atmosphere, no recognizable modern continents, possible partial ocean coverage, debris from recent impacts

coast.description: Ground-level volcanic coastline with primordial ocean
coast.composite_constraints: Black basaltic or komatiite shoreline, dark warm ocean, dense steam, volcanic haze sky, no vegetation, possible lightning, sulfurous atmosphere

impact.composite_constraints: Massive bolide striking surface or ocean, enormous steam/ejecta plume, shock wave, glowing impact melt, debris-filled sky, global dust layer

vent.description: Deep-sea hydrothermal vents and prebiotic environment
vent.composite_constraints: Dark ocean floor, mineral chimneys (black or white smokers), chemical-rich plumes, iron-sulfur deposits, no sunlight, turbulent mixing zones

magma_ocean.description: Global magma ocean phase of earliest Earth
magma_ocean.composite_constraints: Incandescent surface, floating dark solidified crust rafts, thick steam atmosphere, orange-red glow, no liquid water yet

cool_hadean.description: The 'Cool Early Earth' — Hadean with liquid water and calmer conditions
cool_hadean.composite_constraints: Dark ocean under thick overcast sky, rocky islands, warm but not hellish, no modern blue sky, muted and subdued tones

timeline.event: Possible liquid water and earliest continental crust
Oxygen-isotope evidence for surface liquid water
Protocrust differentiation and possible early tectonics
Transition to Eoarchean; earliest possible life
